Topics included in this document are as follows:

·         JobBOSS XML SDK Overview

·         Prerequisites and Assumptions

·         Documentation Conventions

·         Supported Functions

·         Communicating with JobBOSS Using XML

·         JobBOSS XML Document Requirements

·         JBXMLRespond

·         JBXMLRequest

JobBOSS XML SDK Overview

The JobBOSS XML SDK provides a means to use XML formatted data with the JobBOSS database. The SDK implements a method that processes XML documents sent to JobBOSS, allowing you to maintain data (add, modify, and delete records) and query for both individual records and record lists. For example, you can add a collection of customer records, or query to find a list of jobs for a given company name.

XML (Extensible Markup Language) provides a standardized way to describe data exchanged between software applications. Using XML, you can describe almost any data, in a format that is understood by the majority of computers in use today.

These instructions assume that you, as a developer communicating with the JobBOSS database using XML, are already familiar with XML, its format, and its structure. If not, we highly recommend that you acquire some education on the topic, either independently or formally through a class.

Prerequisites and Assumptions

We assume the programmer using this documentation is familiar with basic XML concepts and usage.

The JobBOSS XML SDK uses the Microsoft XML Core Services (MSXML) 4.0, which is installed with the rest of the JobBOSS XML SDK. You can either use the MSXML parser, or another parser, when creating your XML files.

The JobBOSS XML SDK supports the following databases:

·         Microsoft Access

·         Microsoft SQL Server

In order to deploy the JobBOSS XML SDK, you need to have the JobBOSS software installed on your workstation. JobBOSS XML SDK will be automatically installed along with rest of the JobBOSS client software.

Note:  If you have more than one JobBOSS database (that is, you have more than one database alias defined in JobBOSS), the JobBOSS XML SDK will operate against the database you are currently logged into, or the most recent one you were logged into on that workstation.

You need to list elements in your XML documents in the order specified, as there may be later dependencies upon a given functional request.

Documentation Conventions

Conventions used in this document are as follows:

·         Elements can be either required or optional and are listed in their required sequence.

·         Complex elements (those with child elements) are each portrayed as a hyperlink. Further information about child elements (or attributes) is accessible by clicking each link.

·         Simple elements (those without child elements) list their data type after the element name.

·         Most often, each element can appear only once in the sequence; those which can appear more than once are noted as such.

·         Predefined values are listed for elements that have them. Attributes which have values that are predefined by the user are noted as such.

X

 

 

Here are some example requests:

·         JobQueryRq—shows everything about a job.

·         CustomerQueryRq—shows everything about a customer.

·         JobAddRq—adds a new job.

·         CustomerAddRq—adds a new customer.

You can use any ListQueryRq to find data to use elsewhere in other requests (element names correspond to fields in the JobBOSS database).

For queries that could potentially return large amounts of data, be sure to use a filter. For example, if you were to request all jobs with no filter, you might experience performance degradation.

Wildcard Support

For any Query or ListQuery function, you can use the % (percent) symbol as a wildcard character in a query element. For example, this request returns all customers whose identifier begins with “CO”:

<CustomerListQueryRq>

  <QueryFilter>

    <IDFilter>C0%</IDFilter>

  </QueryFilter>

</CustomerListQueryRq>

Sample XML, XSD, and Executable Files 

The following files are installed during the JobBOSS Client installation, in the JobBOSS folder:

File Name

Description

Request.xml

sample request file

Respond.xml

sample response file

Errors.xml

sample response file with errors

JBXML.xsd

schema file

JBXMLMap.xml

a mapping file to JobBOSS objects

Jbinterface.exe

a Delphi executable file

User-Defined Values

User-defined values are specified in JobBOSS’s System Administration module, through the Preferences function, under the User Codes tab. If you try to use the JobBOSS XML SDK to specify a user-defined value which has not been defined in the JobBOSS database as a user code, you will get an error. For example, if you try to add a customer (CustomerAddRq) using a value for TermsCodeRef that has not been defined as a valid terms user code, you will get an error.

On the other hand, if a terms code was valid at the time a record was added, but later this terms code was deleted from the user codes, and you try to modify a customer (CustomerModRq) that uses this term’s code, no error will result.

Communicating with JobBOSS Using XML

Creating the COM Object

To communicate with JobBOSS using XML, you need to use the JobBOSS COM object provided with the SDK. To use the COM object, you need to write some code (using a language such as Delphi, Visual Basic, or some other language) to create the JobBOSS COM object for you. The COM object instance provides you with the interface required to communicate with JobBOSS.

To create and connect to the JobBOSS COM object, you will need to do the following:

1.      Reference the JobBOSS COM object in your application development project. This object (provided by Jbinterface.exe) is called JBRequestProcessor.

2.      Create the JBRequestProcessor object in your code.

3.      Communicate with the JBRequestProcessor object using its ProcessRequest method. The signature of this method is as follows:

·         Takes an XML document (a string, for an incoming XML request file)

·         Returns an XML document (a string, for an outgoing XML response file)

Licensing Requirements

When you use the SDK, it will take up a JobBOSS seat license by requiring a session ID for all XML requests. To obtain a session ID, you need to call CreateSession using a valid JobBOSS user name and password. This returns a Session ID which you can then use for all requests during that session. When all requests have been completed for a session, you need to call CloseSession and pass the corresponding session ID to release the seat license. Any requests that do not have a valid session ID will return an error.

How You Might Use the SDK

Following are some examples of how you might use the SDK:

·         You could insert existing data stored in an Excel spreadsheet into your JobBOSS database.

·         You could write an input form in Visual Basic that accepts your data, submits it to JobBOSS to validate according to its business rules, and then insert this data into your JobBOSS database.

·         You could use an Excel spreadsheet as your input form and to provide user defined validation to your data. This allows you to specify business rules for the Excel form to enforce prior to inserting the data into your JobBOSS database, which then enforces its own validation rules. (To do this, use Visual Basic for Applications to map cells in the Excel spreadsheet to the data with Visual Basic procedures.)

Sample Visual Basic Code 

Following is one example of a Visual Basic code snippet that uses the JobBOSS COM object to process functional requests against the JobBOSS database (this example happens to use MSXML 4.0, although you are not required to do so):

 

Public Sub ProcessXMLRequests()

 

    ' Purpose: Take an XML document containing JobBOSS functional

    ' requests, process its transactions against the JobBOSS database,

    ' and save the resulting response data in an XML file.

    '

    ' Before calling this code, you need to set a reference to

    ' Microsoft XML, v4.0 in your project.

 

    ' Set up space for an object variable for the JobBOSS COM object.

    Dim jb As JBRequestProcessor

 

    ' Set up space for object variables used to contain request and

    ' response strings.

    Dim Request,Response As String

 

    ' Set up space for object variables for MSXML DOMDocument objects.

    ' These object variables represent XML documents that hold response

    ' and request data, respectively.

    Dim ResDoc,ReqDoc As Msxml2.DOMDocument40

    Dim SessionID As String

 

    ' Create the JobBOSS COM object.

    Set jb = New JBRequestProcessor

    SessionID = jb.CreateSession(UserName, Psw, ErrorMsg)

 

    ' Create the MSXML DOMDocument objects for a request and response.

    Set ReqDoc = New Msxml2.DOMDocument40

    Set ResDoc = New Msxml2.DOMDocument40

 

    ' Load request data from a hard-coded file path to an XML file.

    ReqDoc.async = False

    ReqDoc.Load ("C:\MyXML\MyRequestData.xml")

 

    ' Create a string from the data in the ReqDoc object variable and

    ' store it in the Request object variable. In particular, the xml

    ' method is called on the ReqDoc object variable, and the resulting

    ' string is stored in a Request string.

    Request = ReqDoc.xml

 

    ' Pass the Request string to the jb object, which processes the

    ' JobBOSS database transactions as specified in the functional

    ' requests contained in this string of XML data, and return a

    ' Response string (in XML format).

    Response = jb.ProcessRequest(Request)

 

    ' Load the XML response data into the ResDoc object variable. The

    ' LoadXML method (from the MSXML DOM document object) takes as its

    ' argument a Response string.

    ResDoc.LoadXML(Response)

 

    ' Save response data to a hard-coded file path to an XML file.

    ResDoc.save ("C:\MyXML\MyResponseData.xml")

    Jb.CloseSession(SessionID)

 

End Sub

JobBOSS XML Document Requirements

Every XML document used to communicate with the JobBOSS interface must contain the root element JBXML, used to format information according to an XML schema. In addition, the file will contain one of the following elements:

·         JBXMLRequest—each document sent to JobBOSS will contain one JBXMLRequest element, which can contain multiple functional requests.

·         JBXMLRespond—each document received from JobBOSS will contain one JBXMLRespond element, which can contain multiple functional responses (one for each corresponding request).

This document describes all the functional request elements used to send data to JobBOSS, plus selected functional response elements that return data from JobBOSS. To find out about the rest of the response elements, refer to the sample Respond.xml file in your installation.

CAUTION:  In order for your XML document to work properly, you need to enter elements in the order listed. The sequence specified is required to match the JobBOSS XML document schema. If elements are entered in a different sequence, JobBOSS will not process your data.

JBXMLRespond

Upon completing JBXML requests, JobBOSS generates a response file in XML format. The response file includes a root JBXML element and a JBXMLRespond element, plus one or more individual functional response elements. This file sends back the data you supplied in your JBXML request as well as any default data supplied by JobBOSS.

Response Data

The response file provides information which you may need to know in order to create other requests. For example:

·         A LastUpdated element provides the date and time when something was last changed, which can be useful to know when multiple users are maintaining the same data. This element is available for customer add and modification requests (CustomerAddRq and CustomerModRq), among others.

·         Numerous calculated fields are available, such as EstimatedTotalHours, EstimatedTotalTime, ActualTotalTime, RemainingTotalTime, EstimatedTotalCost, and ActualTotalCost.

·         Numeric identifiers (IDs) are sent back for automatically numbered fields.

Error Handling and Reporting

These error codes report the status of an XML request; for each functional request that generates an error, its description is listed in the XML response file:

The description of the error is available in the XML response file.

Code

Description

0 (zero)

success

1

business logic problem (e.g., no customer, customer is over their credit limit, vendor is not in the JobBOSS database, or record already exists)

2

COM object failed

If errors occur, the entire functional request transaction is not committed. For example, say you have an XML request file that includes three customer add requests (that is, three CustomerAddRq elements). If one CustomerAddRq element has errors and the other two don’t, the one with errors will not be added, but the other two will be. Refer to Business Data Maintenance Functions for a list of all functional requests.

Type Formatting

The JobBOSS XML SDK uses ISO standard date and date-time formats, as well as string, boolean, date, int, double, and float types.

The ISO standard date format is: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss

Representation

Description

YYYY

Four-digit year

MM

Two-digit month (01 through 12). January would be 01

DD

Two-digit day of month (01 through 31)

hh

Two digits of hour (00 through 23). Note the am or pm designation is not allowed

mm

Two digits of minute (00 through 59)

ss

Two digits of second (00 through 59)

 

All the components in the date format must be present for a field of type Date, with the punctuation exactly as shown.  If the field is designated as a Date/Time field, then the components of the time format must also be present. Note that the “ T ” appears literally in the string to indicate the beginning of the time element and must be uppercase. You may use “00” for the seconds, minutes or hours when the exact time is unknown.

 

XML requires special characters to be specified as shown:

To insert:

Which is called:

Use this entity reference:

&

ampersand

&amp;

'

apostrophe (single quote)

&apos;

"

quotation mark (double quote)

&quot;

> 

greater-than symbol

&gt;

< 

less-than symbol

&lt;

For example:

XML code:

Result:

<Name>Butch &amp; Sundance</Name>

Butch & Sundance

<Name>Sean O&apos;Neil</Name>

Sean O'Neil

<Name>Larry &quot;Bud&quot; Melman</Name>

Larry "Bud" Melman

<Formula>(x &gt; y) AND (x &lt; z)</Formula>

(x > y) AND (x < z)

Schema Information and XML Usage

The XML schema document (JBXML.xsd) included with your installation specifies the names of elements, the sequence in which they are used, whether they are required or optional, and their types.

Following are some XML usage considerations to keep in mind:

·         Use well-formatted XML, marked with open and close tags (<tagname> and </tagname>).

·         You can validate your XML request against the schema using an XML editor to check for parser errors; this will validate the following:

·         Information is in the correct order

·         Tags have the correct names (names are case-sensitive)

·         Date-time format is correct

You need to know length limits for your data elements. The size of element data is the same as the size of the corresponding fields in your JobBOSS database. You can automatically generate this information about your JobBOSS database using the JobBOSS Database Schema program (c:\Program Files\JobBOSS\DBSchema.exe).

JBXMLRequest

JobBOSS requires an ID and LastUpdated element for any modify request. The method used to obtain the correct LastUpdated date/time is to first send the associated query request. For example, if you wanted to do a MaterialModRq, you would need to send a MaterialQueryRq first to retrieve the LastUpdated value to use in the modify request. The LastUpdated date/time stamp ensures that only one update can be made to a record at one time.

This document lists all possible XML tags which can be used to send data to JobBOSS. Typically, you would need to use only a small subset of these elements at a time.

·         CustomerAddRq                                       Can have multiple.

·         CustomerModRq                                      Can have multiple.

·         CustomerDelRq                                        Can have multiple.

·         CustomerQueryRq                                    Can have multiple.

·         CustomerListQueryRq                              Can have multiple.

·         VendorAddRq                                          Can have multiple.

·         VendorModRq                                          Can have multiple.

·         VendorDelRq                                            Can have multiple.

·         VendorQueryRq                                       Can have multiple.

·         VendorListQueryRq                                 Can have multiple.

·         ServiceOperationAddRq                          Can have multiple.

·         ServiceOperationModRq                          Can have multiple.

·         ServiceOperationDelRq                            Can have multiple.

·         ServiceOperationQueryRq                       Can have multiple.

·         ServiceOperationListQueryRq                 Can have multiple.

·         WorkCenterAddRq                                  Can have multiple.

·         WorkCenterModRq                                  Can have multiple.

·         WorkCenterDelRq                                    Can have multiple.

·         WorkCenterQueryRq                                Can have multiple.

·         WorkCenterListQueryRq                         Can have multiple.

·         StockItemAddRq                                      Can have multiple.

·         StockItemModRq                                     Can have multiple.

·         StockItemDelRq                                       Can have multiple.

·         StockItemQueryRq                                   Can have multiple.

·         StockItemListQueryRq                             Can have multiple.

·         MaterialAddRq                                         Can have multiple.

·         MaterialModRq                                        Can have multiple.

·         MaterialDelRq                                          Can have multiple.

·         MaterialQueryRq                                      Can have multiple.

·         MaterialListQueryRq                                Can have multiple.

·         JobAddRq                                                 Can have multiple.

·         JobModRq                                                Can have multiple.

·         JobDelRq                                                  Can have multiple.

·         JobQueryRq                                              Can have multiple.

·         JobListQueryRq                                        Can have multiple.

·         LocationListQueryRq                               Can have multiple.

·         DepartmentRefListQueryRq                    Can have multiple.

·         CustomerTypeListQueryRq                     Can have multiple.

·         TaxCodeListQueryRq                               Can have multiple.

·         ShipViaListQueryRq                                Can have multiple.

·         AlloyListQueryRq                                    Can have multiple.

·         MaterialClassListQueryRq                       Can have multiple.

·         DiscountLevelListQueryRq                      Can have multiple.

·         TermsListQueryRq                                   Can have multiple.

·         CurrencyListQueryRq                              Can have multiple.

·         SalesCodeListQueryRq                            Can have multiple.

·         LocationTypeListQueryRq                       Can have multiple.

·         WarehouseListQueryRq                           Can have multiple.

·         AdditionalCostListQueryRq                    Can have multiple.

·         StandardTextListQueryRq                       Can have multiple.

·         MaterialConversionQueryRq                    Can have multiple.

·         TimeEntryListQueryRq                            Can have multiple.

·         SalesOrderAddRq                                    Can have multiple.

·         SalesOrderModRq                                    Can have multiple.

·         SalesOrderDelRq                                      Can have multiple.

·         SalesOrderQueryRq                                  Can have multiple.

·         SalesOrderListQueryRq                           Can have multiple.

·         QuoteAddRq                                            Can have multiple.

·         QuoteModRq                                            Can have multiple.

·         QuoteDelRq                                              Can have multiple.

·         QuoteQueryRq                                          Can have multiple.

·         QuoteListQueryRq                                   Can have multiple.

CustomerAddRq

Required Elements

·         CustomerAdd                                            

Optional Elements

·         AddressAdd                                             Can have multiple.

·         ContactAdd                                              Can have multiple.

·         StartUpDocumentAdd                             Can have multiple.

·         SetAddressDefault                                   Can have 3.

CustomerModRq

Required Elements

·         CustomerMod                                            

Optional Elements

·         AddressDel                                               Can have multiple.

·         AddressMod                                             Can have multiple.

·         AddressAdd                                             Can have multiple.

·         ContactDel                                                Can have multiple.

·         ContactMod                                              Can have multiple.

·         ContactAdd                                              Can have multiple.

·         StartUpDocumentAdd                             Can have multiple.

·         StartUpDocumentDel                               Can have multiple.

·         StartUpDocumentMod                             Can have multiple.

·         SetAddressDefault                                   Can have 3.

Required Elements

Required Elements

Required Elements

Required Elements

·         VendorAdd

·         AddressAdd                                             Can have multiple.

·         ContactAdd                                              Can have multiple.

·         ServiceContractAdd                                 Can have multiple.

·         SetAddressDefault                                   Can have 3.

Required Elements

·         VendorMod

·         AddressDel                                               Can have multiple.

·         AddressMod                                             Can have multiple.

·         AddressAdd                                             Can have multiple.

·         ContactDel                                                Can have multiple.

StockItemAddRq

Note:  Stock Item records are used to group Raw Stock materials together.

Required Elements

·         StockItemAdd                                         

·         SetShape                                                  

·         EditAlloy                                                 

Optional Elements

·         EditDimension                                          Can have multiple.

·         WeightFactorMod                                   

·         VendorPartAdd                                        Can have multiple.

StockItemModRq

Required Elements

·         StockItemMod                                         

Optional Elements

·         SetShape                                                  

·         EditAlloy                                                 

·         EditDimension                                          Can have multiple.

·         WeightFactorMod                                   

·         VendorPartMod                                        Can have multiple.

·         VendorPartDelete                                     Can have multiple.

·         VendorPartAdd                                        Can have multiple.

StockItemDelRq

Required Elements

·         StockItemDel

Required Elements

·         StockItemQueryFilter

Required Elements

·         QueryFilter

MaterialAddRq

Note:  This element requires a certain required element, which can be followed by an optional element, then requires more required elements, which can be followed by additional optional elements in sequence.

Required Elements

·         MaterialAdd                                              

Optional Elements

·         SetStockItem                                      Used for raw stock only.
                                                            If not defined, a default Stock Item will be
                                                            created.

Required Elements

·         Choice of:

·         SetShape                                             Used for raw stock only. Default value is
                                                            Round. For JobBOSS 9.0 and later, we
                                                            recommend that you maintain this element
                                                            in StockItemAddRq.

·         AssignRole                                          Used for discrete materials only.
                                                            Default value is‘F’.

Optional Elements

·         AssignUnits                                               

·         EditDimension                                          Can have multiple. Used for raw stock only.

·         EditAlloy                                                  Used for raw stock only. For
                                                                  JobBOSS 9.0 and later, we recommend that
                                                                  you maintain this element in
                                                                  StockItemAddRq.

·         SetRevInformation                                   Used for finished goods only.

·         AddOnHandQty                                       Can have multiple.

·         WeightFactorMod                                    Used for raw stock only.

·         CustomerPartAdd                                     Can have multiple.

·         PriceBreakAdd                                         Can have multiple. Used for discrete materials.

·         PurchaseUnitConvMod                             

·         VendorPartAdd                                        Can have multiple.

MaterialModRq

Note:  This element requires a certain required element, which can be followed by an optional element, then requires more required elements, which can be followed by additional optional elements in sequence.

Required Elements

·         MaterialMod                                              

Optional Elements

·         SetStockItem                                            Used for raw stock only.

Required Elements

·         Choice of:

·         SetShape                                             Used for raw stock only.

·         AssignRole                                         

Optional Elements

·         AssignUnits                                               

·         EditAlloy                                                  Used for raw stock only.

·         EditDimension                                          Can have multiple. Used for raw stock only.

·         SetRevInformation                                   Used for finished goods only.

·         AdjustOnHandQty                                   Can have multiple.

·         WeightFactorMod                                    Used for raw stock only.

·         CustomerPartMod                                    Can have multiple.

·         CustomerPartDelete                                  Can have multiple.

·         CustomerPartAdd                                     Can have multiple.

·         PriceBreakMod                                         Can have multiple. Used for discrete materials.

·         PriceBreakDel                                           Can have multiple. Used for discrete materials.

·         PriceBreakAdd                                         Can have multiple. Used for discrete materials.

·         PurchaseUnitConvMod                             

MaterialDelRq

Required Elements

·         MaterialDel                                                

MaterialQueryRq

Required Elements

·         MaterialQueryFilter                                   

MaterialListQueryRq

Required Elements

·         QueryFilter                                                 

JobAddRq

For assembly structures, you need to add a job via JobAdd for each component in the assembly, as well as for the top level job. Then additionally you need to add a component via ComponentAdd for each component job to tie it to its parent job.

If you want to add components for an assembly job at the time of creating the assembly, you will need to first add all the components as jobs, adding the lowest level components first, and then create the job which uses these components. (Another way to achieve the same result is to add all jobs first using JobAddRq, then use JobModRq to add components where appropriate.) For example, say you have the following component job structure:

Job Number

Level

9800

0

9800-A

1

9800-B

1

9800-C

1

9800-C-1

2

9800-C-2

2

 

To add components 9800-A, 9800-B, and 9800-C to 9800 at the same time as creating 9800, you would need to add 9800-A, 9800-B, and 9800-C as jobs first. Similarly, to add components 9800-C-1 and 9800-C-2 at the same time as creating 9800-C, you would need to add them as jobs first. Thus, the overall order of requests would be as follows (lines are used to separate each functional request):

<JobAddRq>

  <JobAdd>

    <ID>9800-C-1</ID>

  </JobAdd>

</JobAddRq>

<JobAddRq>

  <JobAdd>

    <ID>9800-C-2</ID>

  </JobAdd>

</JobAddRq>

<JobAddRq>

  <JobAdd>

    <ID>9800-A</ID>

  </JobAdd>

</JobAddRq>

<JobAddRq>

  <JobAdd>

    <ID>9800-B</ID>

  </JobAdd>

</JobAddRq>

<JobAddRq>

  <JobAdd>

    <ID>9800-C</ID>

  </JobAdd>

  <ComponentAdd>

    <ComponentJobRef ID="9800-C-1" />

  </ComponentAdd>

  <ComponentAdd>

    <ComponentJobRef ID="9800-C-2" />

  </ComponentAdd>

</JobAddRq>

<JobAddRq>

  <JobAdd>

    <ID>9800</ID>

  </JobAdd>

  <ComponentAdd>

    <ComponentJobRef ID="9800-A" />

  </ComponentAdd>

  <ComponentAdd>

    <ComponentJobRef ID="9800-B" />

  </ComponentAdd>

  <ComponentAdd>

    <ComponentJobRef ID="9800-C" />

  </ComponentAdd>

</JobAddRq>

Required Elements

·         JobAdd

·         SetPriceInfo                                               

·         SetSalesRep

·         ComponentAdd                                        Can have multiple.

CustomerAdd

Required Elements

·         ID                                                              Type: string.

Optional Elements

·         Name                                                         Type: string.

·         PartnerSince                                              Type: date.

·         LeadDays                                                  Type: int.

·         URL                                                          Type: string.

·         EMailAddress                                           Type: string.

·         Status                                                        Type: string.

Status

active

inactive

prospect

warning

pilot

credit hold

reference

·         CreditLimit                                               Type: double.

·         AcceptsBackorders                                   Type: boolean.

·         PrintStatement                                          Type: boolean.

·         SalesRepRef                                              

·         DiscountLevelRef                                      

·         TradingCurrencyRef                                  

·         TermsCodeRef                                           

·         TaxCodeRef                                              

·         CustomerTypeRef                                      

·         ShipViaRef                                                

·         UserValues                                                 

AddressAdd

Business rules for specifying customer and vendor addresses are as follows:

·         You can designate an address as billable, shippable, or both.

·         An address can act as one or more kind of default address (main, billing, and shipping).

·         You need to at least specify a default main address and a default billing address in your system. Specifying a default shipping address is optional.

Note:  If after setting a default address in AddressAdd you later use SetAddressDefault, the more recent value (from SetAddressDefault) is the one that will be used.

Required Elements

·         AddressName                                           Type: string.

Optional Elements

·         Name                                                         Type: string.

·         Line1                                                         Type: string.

·         Line2                                                         Type: string.

·         City                                                           Type: string.

·         State                                                          Type: string.

·         PostalCode                                                Type: string.

·         Country                                                     Type: string.

·         Phone                                                        Type: string.

·         Fax                                                            Type: string.

·         Shippable                                                  Type: boolean.

·         Billable                                                      Type: boolean.

·         DefaultMain                                              Type: boolean.

·         DefaultAccounting                                   Type: boolean.

·         DefaultShipping                                        Type: boolean.

·         PhoneNumber                                           Type: string.

·         PhoneExtension                                        Type: string.

·         EMailAddress                                           Type: string.

·         AddressRef

VendorAdd

Note:  If you plan to add a service contract for a new vendor, you need to add the service contract first, and then add the vendor that uses the service contract.

·         ID                                                              Type: string.

Optional Elements

·         PartnerSince                                              Type: date.

Inactive

VendorPartMod

Note:  You cannot use VendorPartMod to change the vendor ID for a vendor part. Instead, use VendorPartDelete to delete the vendor part and then use VendorPartAdd to add it again with the correct vendor ID.

Required Elements

·         ID                                                              Type: string. Internal ID for vendor part.

·         LastUpdated                                             Type: dateTime.

Optional Elements

·         VendorReferenceNbr                               Type: string.

·         LeadDays                                                  Type: int.

·         PriceUnit                                                   Type: string.

·         SellPrice                                                    Type: double.

JobAdd

To have JobBOSS generate automatically numbered IDs for you (instead of your having to explicitly specify them), enter a blank character as the value for the ID. For example:

- <JobAddRq>

- <JobAdd>

  <ID> </ID>

(Optional elements for JobAdd go here…)

  </JobAdd>

(Optional elements for JobAddRq go here…)

  </JobAddRq>

Required Elements

·         ID                                                              Type: string.

Optional Elements

·         BuildtoStock                                             Type: boolean.

·         CustomerPO                                             Type: string.

·         CustomerPOLine                                      Type: string.

·         TimeandMaterial                                       Type: boolean.

·         Quote                                                        Type: string.

·         Status                                                        Type: string.

Value

Pending

Active

Hold

Complete

Closed

Template

·         Comments                                                 Type: string.

·         Priority                                                      Type: int.

·         CertsReqd                                                 Type: boolean.

·         Notes                                                         Type: string.

·         LeadDays                                                  Type: int.

·         OrderDate                                                 Type: date.

·         OrderQty                                                  Type: int.

·         ShipViaRef                                                

·         PricingCalculator                                       

·         UserValues                                                 

·         Source                                                       Type: string.

Value

Description

SDK

Specifies the data source is XML formatted data added using the SDK.

AdditionalCost

Required Elements

·         Description                                                Type: string.

·         Amount                                                     Type: double.

MaterialConversionQuery

This element converts a value from one unit of measure to another.

When you specifiy an initial amount and unit, along with a target unit of measure, an amount in the target unit of measure is returned with the target unit of measure.

For example, if you specify a value of 1 foot (amount is 1 and unit is foot), and the target unit of measure is inches, the amount returned would be 12.

Required Elements

·         ID                                                              Type: string.

·         Amount                                                     Type: double.

·         Unit                                                           Type: string.

·         TargetUnit                                                 Type: string.

·         IDFilter                                                     Type: string.

·         BeginDateFilter                                        Type: dateTime.

·         EndDateFilter                                           Type: dateTime.

AdditionalChargeProperty

Optional Elements

·         Amount                                                     Type: double.

·         Description                                                Type: string. Valid values are user defined.

·         Commissionable                                        Type: boolean.

·         SalesCodeRef                                            

OrderTakerRef

Note:  This is the same as an employee reference, representing an employee.

Required Attributes

·         Name = “ID”                                            Type: string

PickMaterial

If you specify a location for this element, the system will pick materials from that location. If not enough materials are present in the specified location, an error is returned.

If you don’t specify a location, the system will go through and pick materials from all available locations in order from the one with the highest quantity on hand to the lowest.

Optional Elements

·         LocationRef                                               

·         Quantity                                                    Type: double.

·         Lot                                                            Type: string.

·         PickDate                                                   Type: dateTime.

OrderTakerRef

Required Attributes

·         Name = “ID”                                            Type: string.

QuoteAddRq

Note:  This element requires a certain required element, which can be followed by an optional element, then requires more required elements, which can be followed by an additional optional element in sequence.

Required Elements

·         QuoteAdd                                                

Optional Elements

·         QuoteSetUpCustomerInfo                        

Required Elements

·         QuoteLineItemAdd                                  Can have multiple.

·         QuoteQuantityAdd                                   Can have multiple.

Optional Elements

·         QuoteAdditionalChargeAdd                    Can have multiple.

QuoteModRq

Required Elements

·         QuoteMod                                                 

Optional Elements

·         QuoteSetUpCustomerInfo                        

·         QuoteLineItemAdd                                  Can have multiple.

·         QuoteLineItemMod                                  Can have multiple.

·         QuoteQuantityAdd                                   Can have multiple.

·         QuoteQuantityMod                                  Can have multiple.

·         QuoteAdditionalChargeAdd                    Can have multiple.

·         QuoteAdditionalChargeMod                   Can have multiple.

QuoteDelRq

Required Elements

·         QuoteDel                                                   

QuoteQueryRq

Required Elements

·         QuoteQueryFilter                                       

QuoteListQueryRq

Required Elements

·         QueryFilter                                                 

QuoteAdd

To have JobBOSS generate automatically numbered IDs for you (instead of your having to explicitly specify them), enter a blank character as the value for the ID. For example:

- <QuoteAddRq>

- <QuoteAdd>

  <ID> </ID>

(Optional elements for QuoteAdd go here…)

  </QuoteAdd>

(Optional elements for QuoteAddRq go here…)

  </QuoteAddRq

Required Elements

·         ID                                                              Type: string.

Optional Elements

·         Reference                                                  Type: string.

·         QuotedBy                                                 Type: string.

·         SubmittedDate                                          Type: date.

·         ExpirationDate                                          Type: date.

·         RFQDate                                                  Type: date.

·         QuoteDate                                                Type: date.

·         StatusDate                                                Type: date.

·         Comments                                                 Type: string.

·         Notes                                                         Type: string.

·         LeadDays                                                  Type: int.

·         CertsReqd                                                 Type: boolean.

·         Priority                                                      Type: int.

·         Status                                                        Type: string.

Value

Active

Lost

Expired

Hold

Template

Won

Pending

No Quote

SetAddressDefault

Note:  If you first set a default address in AddressAdd, then later use SetAddressDefault, the more recent value (from SetAddressDefault) is the one that will be used.

Required Elements

·         ShipToID                                                  Type: string.

·         DefaultType                                              Type: string.

Value

Main

Ship

Bill

SDK

Specifies the data source is XML formatted data added using the SDK.

QuoteQuantityMod

Required Elements

·         ID                                                              Type: string.

·         LineItemID                                               Type: string.

·         LastUpdated                                             Type: dateTime.

·         QuotedQuantity                                        Type: int.

Optional Elements

·         DecimalPlaces                                           Type: double.

·         QuotePrice                                                Type: double.

·         OrderUnit                                                 Type: string.

·         PriceUnit                                                   Type: string.

·         PriceUnitConversion                                 Type: double.

·         Yield                                                         Type: double.

·         Notes                                                         Type: string.

QuoteAdditionalChargeMod

Required Elements

·         ID                                                              Type: string.

·         LineItemID                                               Type: string.

·         LastUpdated                                             Type: dateTime.

Optional Elements

·         Amount                                                     Type: double.

·         Description                                                Type: string. Valid values are user defined.

·         Commissionable                                        Type: boolean.

·         JobRevenue                                               Type: boolean.

·         Notes                                                         Type: string.

·         SalesCodeRef                                            

StatusReasonRef

Required Attributes

·         Name = “ID”                                            Type: string. Valid values are user defined.

QuoteDel

·         ID                                                              Type: string.

QuoteQueryFilter

·         ID                                                              Type: string.
